title: Employer evidence for an Agreement to Recruit (ATR) :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Employer evidence for an Agreement to Recruit
If you are a Recognised Seasonal Employer (RSE) who is applying for an Agreement to Recruit (ATR) you must supply evidence to help us process your application.
As a (RSE) of or workers, you need to send the following evidence with your Agreement to Recruit (ATR) application.

## Evidence of agreements and contracts with third parties
- a copy of the employment agreement you will offer overseas workers showing the required information
- copies of contracts for service you hold with third parties — if required in your application.
Minimum requirements for RSE worker employment agreements

## Evidence of pay deduction
You must supply evidence that the worker's deductions:
- comply with relevant New Zealand employment legislation
- are for a specified purpose and for actual, reasonable, verifiable expenses
- are no more than those from New Zealanders (in comparable circumstances).
Agreeing to voluntary pay deductions when you work on an RSE visa

## Evidence of accommodation
You must supply evidence showing:
- you own the accommodation — if it is in Hawke's Bay or the Bay of Plenty it needs to show the purchase date, or
- the accommodation meets requirements if you do not own it, for example a rental agreement or similar, booking information, or council consent information (if it was purpose-built for horticulture and viticulture workers).
Providing pastoral care and accommodation for RSE workers

## Evidence you agree to pay repatriation costs
You must include a signed letter of guarantee that states you agree to:
- pay a fixed amount of repatriation costs to send a worker who breaks their visa conditions home
- pay a fixed amount towards repatriating any worker who breaks their visa conditions — note, the repatriation costs can be anything up to NZD $3,000 for each worker (this includes any living and accommodation costs).
You must tell us when your RSE worker:
- is suspended or dismissed
- breaks their visa conditions.
